generated_oml:
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor providing torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) conversion from electrical current (I_M) to mechanical motion. Part of the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component transmitting torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) between motor and cycloidal gearbox. Part of the first axis transmission system."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox providing high torque transmission (τ_out) and angular velocity (ω_out) with reduced backlash. Constrained to [-90°, 90°] operational range with max speed 75°/s."
]

instance SINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller executing control algorithms (position/speed/acceleration inputs) and adjusting motor current (I_M) to achieve desired motion for subtractive manufacturing tasks."
]

instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular position data for motor shaft (r_M)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing real-time angular position data for gearbox output shaft (r_G)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) supplied to the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series data streamed via MQTT from motor encoder containing raw angular position measurements (r_M) for friction analysis."
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series data streamed via MQTT from secondary encoder containing raw angular position measurements (r_G) for backlash and elasticity analysis."
]

instance CurrentSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
    base:desc "Time-series data streamed via MQTT from current sensor containing measured input current (I_M) for torque computation."
]

instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (bdd) defining structural components (Motor, Gearbox) and their interconnections (rotational/current flows) for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (ibd) instantiating the first axis as a FirstAxis system composed of Motor, Gearwheel, and CycloidalGearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance FrictionlessGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink/MATLAB model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction effects for baseline comparison."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink/MATLAB friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction dynamics for torque/velocity compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink/MATLAB friction model implementing Stribeck effect for boundary lubrication conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink/MATLAB friction model implementing Dahl's hysteresis model for pre-sliding behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink/MATLAB friction model implementing LuGre dynamics for comprehensive friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MotorSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink/MATLAB model converting input current (I_M) to motor torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) using manufacturer parameters."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredInputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw measured input current (I_M) from current sensor used as input for motor simulation and friction model validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MotorEncoderRawData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw angular position data (r_M) from motor encoder used for velocity/acceleration derivation and model validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SecondaryEncoderRawData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw angular position data (r_G) from secondary encoder used for gearbox output analysis and backlash detection."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed motor torque (τ_M) derived from I_M using manufacturer dynamics models for friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredInputCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed motor angular velocity (ω_M) derived from motor encoder data (r_M) for simulation input."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderRawData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedGearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed gearbox torque (τ_G) derived from motor torque (τ_M) and gear ratio for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedMotorTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedGearboxVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed gearbox angular velocity (ω_G) derived from secondary encoder data (r_G) for output analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderRawData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionCompensatedTorqueMotor : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated motor torque (τ̃r_M) output from friction models for motion accuracy improvement."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedMotorTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionCompensatedTorqueGearbox : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated gearbox torque (τ̃r_G) output from friction models for control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedGearboxTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionCompensatedTorqueOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated output torque (τ̃r_out) from cycloidal gearbox for final motion accuracy assessment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedGearboxTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time unidirectional streaming of sensor data (I_M, r_M, r_G) from SINUMERIK 840D controller to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelIdentificationService, DataVisualizationService, ModelTraceabilityService
]

instance SimulinkTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink/MATLAB environment executing friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and motor/gearbox simulations in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelIdentificationService, DeviationAnalysisService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool (IBM Rational Rhapsody/Cameo) for structural/behavioral modeling of robot axis components and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelTraceabilityService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell providing semantic linking between data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G) and their corresponding models (Motor, Gearbox)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelTraceabilityService, DataVisualizationService
]

instance UnifiedDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified visualization dashboard displaying time-series data (measured vs. simulated torque/velocity) for friction analysis and deviation visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance FrictionModelIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models to detect deviations in joint behavior using Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, and LuGre models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, ModelValidationInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard service displaying time-series data traces (measured vs. simulated torque/velocity) with semantic traceability of friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTrendsInsight, DeviationVisualizationInsight
]

instance ModelTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service linking data points to their sources (sensors/models) and processing components (Simulation_M, Simulation_GW, Simulation_GB) via UML-based Digital Shadow Reference Model."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSourceInsight, ProcessingProvenanceInsight
]

instance DeviationAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service computing discrepancies between reference data (PT measurements) and simulated data (DS models) to quantify friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionInaccuracyInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service detecting wear patterns (increasing friction over time) to schedule maintenance based on real-time trends and deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides WearPatternInsight, MaintenanceScheduleAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service suggesting parameter updates for SINUMERIK 840D controller to compensate for identified friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControllerParameterSuggestionAction
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static/kinetic/viscous friction components in the gearbox via model deviations and real-time data comparison."
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) against measured data to assess accuracy and detect model limitations."
]

instance RealTimeTrendsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time trends analysis of torque ripple, velocity deviations, and friction effects for operational monitoring."
]

instance DeviationVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of discrepancies between measured and simulated data to highlight friction-induced inaccuracies."
]

instance DataSourceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic traceability of data sources (sensors, models) and their processing provenance for model validation."
]

instance ProcessingProvenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Documentation of data processing chain from raw sensor measurements to final simulated outputs for auditability."
]

instance FrictionInaccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30%) affecting motion accuracy in the first vertical axis."
]

instance WearPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of wear patterns through increasing friction over time, indicating potential mechanical degradation."
]

instance ControllerParameterSuggestionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested parameter updates for SINUMERIK 840D controller to compensate for identified friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of maintenance schedules based on detected wear patterns and friction deviations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DS is **passive** (no direct feedback to PT per Kritzinger et al., 2020). However, the **insights derived from the DS** (e.g., friction model parameters, deviation analysis between simulated and measured data) can be **manually integrated** into the **SINUMERIK 840D controller** to refine motion control algorithms. This is an **indirect interaction**, requiring human intervention to update control logic based on DS outputs."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DS operates in **real-time** with **sub-millisecond latency** for sensor data acquisition (MQTT streaming) and **millisecond-scale processing** for friction model computations (Simulink/MATLAB). The **twinning synchronization** occurs at the **operational time-scale** of the robot (e.g., during machining tasks), with **continuous data updates** to reflect dynamic changes (e.g., wear, temperature, load). The DS does not support **predictive twinning** (future states) but focuses on **current-state analysis**."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DS constellation consists of:
  1. **Physical Twin (PT)**: MABI MAX robot with **first axis components (motor, gearboxes, encoders)** and **SINUMERIK 840D controller**.
  2. **Digital Shadow (DS)**:
     - **Data Sources**: MQTT-streamed sensor data (**I_M**, encoder outputs).
     - **Processing Layer**: Simulink models (**Simulation M**, **Simulation GW**, **Simulation GB**) executing friction computations.
     - **Model Layer**: SysML structural models and Simulink behavioral models.
     - **Output Layer**: Dashboard for **visualization and analysis**.
  3. **Orchestration**:
     - **One-way data flow** from PT → DS (no feedback loop).
     - **Centralized processing** in a **single DS instance** (not distributed).
     - **Traceability links** between **data traces**, **processing components**, and **models** via **DSRM associations** (e.g., **originates_from**, **uses**)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DS covers the **operational phase** of the PT, focusing on **as-operated** behavior (not as-designed or as-manufactured). Key stages include:
  1. **Design Phase**: Creation of **SysML structural models** (Block Definition Diagrams) and **Simulink behavioral models** for friction simulation.
  2. **Implementation Phase**: Deployment of **MQTT-based data acquisition**, **Simulink processing pipelines**, and **dashboard visualization**.
  3. **Operational Phase**: **Real-time friction monitoring** and **deviation analysis** during robot operation (e.g., machining). The DS does not support **retrospective analysis** of past states but provides **live insights** for current operations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - **Deviation Analysis**: Compare **simulated data (ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out)** with **measured data (r_M, r_G, r_out)** to quantify friction effects.
    - **Parameter Tuning**: Adjust friction model parameters (e.g., Coulomb coefficient, Stribeck curve) to minimize discrepancies.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Encoder Discrepancy**: Use **difference between motor and secondary encoders** to infer **elasticity/friction** in the gearbox.
    - **Manufacturer Data**: Leverage **robot-specific parameters** (e.g., gear ratios, motor constants) for accurate torque/velocity calculations.
  - **Verification**:
    - **Traceability**: Each **data point** is linked to its **source (sensor/model)** and **processing step** via **DSRM**.
    - **Dashboard Visualization**: Provides **real-time validation** of model accuracy.
  - **Limitations**:
    - **No access to intermediate gearbox output** (only motor and final output are measured).
    - **Assumption of linear dynamics** for derived torque/velocity (per manufacturer models)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** between PT and DS is established via:
  - **Protocol**: **MQTT** (lightweight, publish-subscribe) for **real-time sensor data streaming** from the **SINUMERIK 840D controller**.
  - **Data Format**: **Time-series arrays** of floating-point values (e.g., **τ [Nm]**, **ω [rad/s]**, **I_M [A]**).
  - **Network Architecture**:
    - **Centralized**: DS processes data on a **single server** (not distributed).
    - **No direct PT-DS feedback**: Data flows **unidirectionally** (PT → DS).
  - **Latency**: **Sub-millisecond** for sensor data acquisition; **millisecond-scale** for Simulink processing."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DS is hosted on a **local server** (not cloud-based) with the following deployment aspects:
  - **Software Stack**:
    - **Simulink/MATLAB**: Executes **friction models** and **data processing**.
    - **MQTT Broker**: Manages **sensor data streaming** (e.g., Mosquitto).
    - **Dashboard**: Built with **web-based tools** (e.g., JavaScript/HTML) for visualization.
  - **Hardware Requirements**:
    - **Real-time capable PC** for Simulink execution.
    - **Network connectivity** to the **SINUMERIK 840D controller** via MQTT.
  - **No edge deployment**: Processing occurs **centrally** (not on robot controllers)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DS does **not explicitly integrate** with external systems in the provided documents. However, potential horizontal integration points include:
  - **Manufacturer Data**: Uses **MABI Robotic AG-provided parameters** (e.g., gear ratios, motor constants) for dynamics modeling.
  - **Standardized Interfaces**: MQTT and **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** could enable **future integration** with:
    - **Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)** systems for **maintenance scheduling**.
    - **Other Digital Twins (DTs)** in a **factory-wide IoT ecosystem**.
    - **Simulation Tools** (e.g., ANSYS, SolidWorks) for **multi-physics validation**.
  - **No explicit APIs or protocols** for external data exchange are documented."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy considerations are **not explicitly addressed** in the document. However, implied aspects include:
  - **Data Sources**:
    - **Sensor Data**: Owned by the **robot manufacturer (MABI Robotic AG)** or **end-user (factory)**.
    - **Manufacturer Parameters**: Likely **proprietary** (e.g., gear ratios, motor constants).
  - **Privacy Risks**:
    - **No encryption** or **access control** mechanisms are mentioned for MQTT data streams.
    - **No anonymization** of operational data (e.g., torque/velocity traces).
  - **Compliance**: Assumes adherence to **industrial data security standards** (e.g., IEC 62443) but does not specify."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DS adheres to the following **standards and frameworks**:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Defines **concepts (Purpose, DataTrace, Processing, Model)** and their **associations** (e.g., **originates_from**, **uses**).
  - **SysML (OMG Standard)**: For **structural/behavioral modeling** of the robot axis.
  - **MQTT (OASIS Standard)**: For **lightweight, real-time data streaming**.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: For **semantic interoperability** between data traces and models (per IEC 63278/ISO 23224).
  - **Simulink/MATLAB**: Uses **model-based design** standards for **executable simulations**.
  - **No explicit compliance** with **Digital Twin standards** (e.g., NIST, ISO 23224) is mentioned."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are **not explicitly detailed**, but implied risks include:
  - **Data Integrity**:
    - **No tamper-proof mechanisms** for MQTT data streams (vulnerable to spoofing if unsecured).
    - **No validation** of sensor data authenticity (e.g., encoder readings).
  - **Operational Safety**:
    - **No remote execution**: DS is passive (no direct PT control), but **manual integration of insights** into the **SINUMERIK 840D controller** could introduce risks if misconfigured.
    - **No fail-safes** for **dashboard visualization errors** (e.g., incorrect friction model parameters leading to wrong control decisions).
  - **Compliance Gaps**:
    - **No mention of cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., TLS for MQTT, authentication for dashboard access).
    - **No safety certification** (e.g., ISO 13849 for machinery safety)."
]
